items.briefing:
The Australian Signals Directorate has announced it will retire the Essential Eight cybersecurity framework within two years and is currently consulting on a replacement. Australian organisations using the Essential Eight as their security baseline should monitor this process closely, as changes could affect compliance requirements. Separately, Apple Mac users should be aware of a newly identified macOS vulnerability that could allow users — or malicious software — to bypass security tools.

With the FIFA World Cup underway, staff should be cautious of scam emails, fake ticketing sites, and phishing attempts exploiting World Cup themes. **Tip:** Be sceptical of any unsolicited emails offering World Cup deals or prize giveaways — go directly to official websites rather than clicking links.

featured.title: ASD to retire Essential Eight within two years, consults on replacement
featured.quote: Essential Eight retirement signals major shift in Australia's cybersecurity guidance framework.
